title: BUG: EntityId::from_bytes is pub without test gate, bypasses INV-FERR-012 (DEFECT-I-003)
description:
**Observed**: EntityId::from_bytes([u8; 32]) is pub with no cfg(test) or feature gate. Accepts arbitrary bytes that are NOT a BLAKE3 hash. Production code could use it to create EntityIds that violate content-addressed identity.
**Expected**: INV-FERR-012: EntityId must be derived from content via BLAKE3. The from_bytes escape hatch should be restricted to test builds.
**Reproducer**: In production code: EntityId::from_bytes([0u8; 32]) compiles and runs, creating a non-content-addressed ID.
**Root cause**: Generators require from_bytes for arbitrary ID space coverage. But it's unrestricted pub.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-012
**File(s)**: ferratom/src/datom.rs
**Fix**: Gate behind #[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-utils"))] or add prominent #[doc(hidden)] with safety comment. Pragmatic choice: feature flag "test-utils" since generators.rs is in a separate crate.
close_reason: Fixed: EntityId::from_bytes, AgentId::from_seed, TxId::new gated behind #[cfg(any(test, feature = test-utils))]. ferratom Cargo.toml has test-utils feature. ferratomic-core enables it. INV-FERR-012.

title: BUG: merge() discards schema and resets epoch to 0 (DEFECT-I-001)
description:
**Observed**: merge() calls Store::from_datoms() which constructs with Schema::empty() and epoch: 0. A merged store loses all schema attributes and epoch history from both inputs.
**Expected**: INV-FERR-009: merged store should union schemas (all attributes from both). INV-FERR-007: epoch should be max(epoch_a, epoch_b).
**Reproducer**: merge two genesis stores where one has schema evolution — merged store has empty schema.
**Root cause**: Store::from_datoms() is a convenience constructor for tests that doesn't preserve schema/epoch. merge() uses it as its sole construction path.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-009, INV-FERR-007
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/merge.rs, ferratomic-core/src/store.rs
**Fix**: Add Store::from_datoms_with_context(datoms, schema, epoch) or Store::merge_from(a, b) that unions datoms AND schemas, takes max epoch.
close_reason: Fixed: merge() now uses Store::from_merge() which unions schemas and takes max epoch. Regression test test_bug_bd_10p_merge_preserves_schema and test_bug_bd_10p_merge_preserves_epoch both pass.

title: BUG: Missing regression test for retract_datom (DEFECT-II-002)
description:
**Observed**: retract_datom() added in bd-79n fix but no test_bug_bd_79n_retract_datom regression test. Bug triage protocol requires every fix to include a named regression test.
**Expected**: INV-FERR-018, INV-FERR-029. Test that retract_datom creates Op::Retract datom, can be committed and transacted, and store grows.
**Reproducer**: No test exists for retract_datom.
**Root cause**: Omission during fix.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-018, INV-FERR-029
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/writer.rs
**Fix**: Add test_bug_bd_79n_retract_datom that builds a retraction transaction, commits it, transacts it, and verifies the datom has Op::Retract.
close_reason: Fixed: test_bug_bd_79n_retract_datom and test_bug_bd_79n_mixed_assert_retract added. Both pass. INV-FERR-018, INV-FERR-029.

title: Add Lean model fidelity documentation to spec/00-preamble.md
description:
**What**: Document the structural gap between Lean model and Rust implementation in spec preamble §23.0.4.
**Why**: Cleanroom Finding A: the Lean model operates on Finset Datom with Nat fields. The gap (no hashing, no serialization, no epochs in LIVE, no crypto in VKN) is a design decision per ADR-FERR-007, but undocumented. Agents may over-trust Lean proof coverage.
**Acceptance**:
1. §23.0.4 has a "Model Fidelity" subsection listing what is abstracted: hashing (INV-FERR-012), serialization (INV-FERR-013), epoch ordering (INV-FERR-029), crypto (INV-FERR-051..055).
2. Each abstracted aspect names the verification layer that covers it instead (proptest, integration, Kani).
3. No new Lean code written — this is documentation only.
**File(s)**: spec/00-preamble.md
**Depends on**: None.
close_reason: Merged into bd-3ej (doc fixes). Model fidelity documentation added as acceptance criterion 6 in bd-3ej.

title: DEFECT-007: INV-FERR-004 missing strict growth for new datoms
close_reason: Superseded by fix task bd-1l9

title: STYLE: leak_arc_str function name suggests unsafe memory leak (DEFECT-I-012)
description:
**Observed**: Function named leak_arc_str at store.rs:505 just calls arc.as_ref() — a normal borrow. The name and 8-line doc about "artificially extended lifetime" are misleading. No leak occurs.
**Expected**: Clear naming per AGENTS.md naming conventions.
**Reproducer**: Read store.rs:505 — name suggests Box::leak or similar.
**Root cause**: Over-documented simple operation.
**Affected INV**: None (code clarity)
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/store.rs
**Fix**: Inline the .as_ref() call at the 2 call sites, delete the function.
close_reason: Fixed: leak_arc_str deleted, replaced with inline kw.as_ref() call.

title: BUG: Kani harnesses couple to internal fields across crate boundaries
description:
**Observed**: `ferratomic-verify/kani/*.rs` accesses raw fields such as `store.indexes`, `store.datoms`, `d.tx_epoch`, `datom.entity`, and uses a `Datom { .. }` literal from another crate.
**Expected**: Verification code in a separate crate should depend on public accessors like `datoms()`, `indexes()`, `attribute()`, `value()`, and public constructors/builders, preserving the minimal public API discipline in AGENTS.md.
**Reproducer**: Static inspection of `ferratomic-verify/kani/store_views.rs`, `schema_identity.rs`, `sharding.rs`, and `durability.rs`.
**Root cause**: The Kani harnesses mirrored white-box spec snippets instead of the verification crate's black-box/public API style.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-005, INV-FERR-006, INV-FERR-012, INV-FERR-017, INV-FERR-020
close_reason: Superseded by audit beads bd-2p4, bd-2sr, bd-2jt. Internal field coupling addressed when harnesses are rewritten to use public API.

title: Add INV-FERR-004 strict growth theorem for new datoms
description:
**What**: Prove d ∉ s → (apply_tx s d).card = s.card + 1.
**Why**: INV-FERR-004 Level 0 requires strict growth for new datoms. Current apply_monotone only proves ≤.
**Acceptance**:
1. theorem apply_strict_growth proves card increase by exactly 1 for new datoms.
2. Proof uses Finset.card_insert_of_not_mem or equivalent.
3. lake build Ferratomic.Store passes.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-verify/lean/Ferratomic/Store.lean
**Depends on**: None.
close_reason: apply_strict_growth: d ∉ s → card = card + 1, via Finset.card_insert_of_notMem

title: BUG: transact() does not stamp real TxId on datoms (DEFECT-I-002)
description:
**Observed**: Datoms enter Store with placeholder TxId(0,0,0) from Transaction builder. transact() inserts them as-is without replacing the placeholder. Every datom in the store has tx == TxId(0,0,0).
**Expected**: INV-FERR-015: transact() should create a real TxId (from HybridClock::tick() or at minimum from the epoch) and re-stamp each datom. writer.rs doc says "The real TxId will be stamped by the Store at transact time."
**Reproducer**: Store::genesis().transact(tx) — check tx field of inserted datoms, all are TxId(0,0,0).
**Root cause**: transact() increments epoch but never constructs TxId from it or re-creates datoms with real timestamps.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-015, INV-FERR-007, INV-FERR-006 (snapshot epoch filtering depends on datom tx)
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/store.rs
**Fix**: In transact(), generate TxId::with_agent(epoch, 0, transaction.agent()) and reconstruct each datom with the real TxId before inserting.
close_reason: Fixed: transact() generates TxId::with_agent(epoch, 0, agent) and re-stamps all datoms. Regression test test_bug_bd_1n6_transact_stamps_real_tx_id passes.

title: Fix proptest atomicity (post-commit snapshot) and observer trigger rate (CR-019, CR-020)
description:
**What**: transaction_atomicity takes snapshot AFTER commit (always sees all datoms, never partial). observer_never_regresses observe_points range 0..20 may never match short txn lists, making test vacuous.
**Why**: INV-FERR-006, INV-FERR-011. Tests cannot detect the bugs they claim to verify.
**Acceptance**:
1. transaction_atomicity documented as single-threaded limitation; comment added pointing to Stateright for concurrent atomicity.
2. observer_never_regresses constrains observe_points to 0..txns.len() and asserts >= 2 observations.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-verify/proptest/index_properties.rs, ferratomic-verify/proptest/schema_properties.rs
**Depends on**: bd-85j.6 (Types must exist).

title: BUG: Vacuous transact tests — silent skip on failure
description: Proptest properties used if-let-Ok/let-_ for transact, passing vacuously if transact always Err. FIXED: replaced with .expect(). Affected: INV-FERR-004,005,006,007,011
close_reason: Fixed: replaced if-let-Ok/let-_ with .expect() in all transact-dependent proptests

title: Add INV-FERR-029 live_bounded theorem via foldl induction
description:
**What**: Prove (live_view_model datoms).card ≤ datoms.length via generalized foldl accumulator induction.
**Why**: INV-FERR-029 spec includes live_bounded (with sorry). Currently omitted entirely.
**Acceptance**:
1. theorem live_bounded_aux: generalized with init accumulator.
2. theorem live_bounded derived with init=∅.
3. Proof uses Finset.card_union_le and Finset.card_le_card + sdiff_subset.
4. lake build Ferratomic.Performance passes.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-verify/lean/Ferratomic/Performance.lean
**Depends on**: None.
close_reason: live_bounded proven via generalized foldl induction (live_bounded_aux)

title: Implement Semilattice trait on Store (connect algebraic declaration to implementation)
description:
**What**: impl Semilattice for Store { fn merge(&self, other: &Self) -> Self }. Connect the algebraic trait declaration to the merge implementation.
**Why**: INV-FERR-001..003. The Semilattice trait in traits.rs declares the contract. The merge function in merge.rs implements it. But Store does not impl the trait — the algebraic contract is disconnected from the type system.
**Acceptance**:
1. impl Semilattice for Store delegates to merge::merge (or from_merge).
2. impl ContentAddressed for Datom delegates to content_hash().
3. Code that uses trait bounds (e.g., fn requires_semilattice<T: Semilattice>(x: &T)) works with Store.
4. cargo check clean.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/store.rs (or a new traits_impl.rs)
**Depends on**: None.

title: Attempt full workspace compilation (cargo check --workspace) and fix compilation errors
description:
**What**: Run cargo check --workspace. Fix compilation errors in ferratomic-verify and ferratomic-datalog. This is the Phase 4a acceptance gate checkpoint.
**Why**: Phase 4a acceptance: "ALL Phase 2 tests pass." Currently ferratomic-verify cannot compile because it references APIs that don't exist or have moved. Compilation is the prerequisite for running any test.
**Acceptance**:
1. cargo check --workspace succeeds with 0 errors.
2. cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings succeeds with 0 warnings.
3. All compilation fixes are minimal (type/import changes, not logic rewrites).
**File(s)**: ferratomic-verify/src/generators.rs, ferratomic-verify integration + proptest + kani + stateright files, ferratomic-datalog/src/*.rs
**Depends on**: Integration test API migration bead.

title: Add INV-FERR-051 key-binding theorem for Ed25519
description:
**What**: Add ed25519_key_binding axiom and signed_key_binding theorem to VKN.lean.
**Why**: INV-FERR-051 spec requires three theorems (correctness, tamper detection, key binding). Key binding is missing.
**Acceptance**:
1. axiom ed25519_key_binding: public_key sk ≠ public_key sk' → verify with sk' fails.
2. theorem signed_key_binding derived from axiom.
3. lake build Ferratomic.VKN passes.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-verify/lean/Ferratomic/VKN.lean
**Depends on**: None.
close_reason: ed25519_key_binding axiom + signed_key_binding theorem added

title: Fix VKN verify_vkc Lean model: check context + calibration proofs (CR-003)
description:
**What**: verify_vkc currently only checks signature (verify_tx). Spec requires 3 checks: (1) signature, (2) context proofs verify against store_root, (3) calibration proofs verify against store_root.
**Why**: INV-FERR-055 (VKC). A VKC with valid signature but fabricated context/calibration data passes the current model. The three-part soundness claimed in the file header is absent.
**Acceptance**:
1. verify_vkc checks verify_tx AND context_root match AND calibration_root match.
2. vkc_authentic theorem updated to reflect 3-part verification.
3. lake build succeeds with zero sorry.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-verify/lean/Ferratomic/VKN.lean
**Depends on**: None.

title: Add INV-FERR-039 selective_merge_idemp theorem
description:
**What**: Prove selective_merge(selective_merge(l,r,p), r, p) = selective_merge(l,r,p).
**Why**: INV-FERR-039 spec includes idempotency. Currently omitted.
**Acceptance**:
1. theorem selective_merge_idemp proven via union_assoc + union_self.
2. lake build Ferratomic.Federation passes.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-verify/lean/Ferratomic/Federation.lean
**Depends on**: None.
close_reason: selective_merge_idemp proven via union_assoc + union_self

title: Enrich INV-FERR-013 checkpoint model with non-identity encode/decode
description:
**What**: Replace id-function checkpoint model with encode to List (Nat×Nat×Nat×Nat×Bool) and decode back. Prove roundtrip.
**Why**: INV-FERR-013 current proof is id∘id=rfl — unfalsifiable. A non-identity model forces the proof to demonstrate field preservation.
**Acceptance**:
1. checkpoint_encode maps DatomStore to List of tuples via Multiset.map.
2. checkpoint_decode reconstructs DatomStore from tuple list.
3. checkpoint_roundtrip proves decode(encode(s)) = s.
4. lake build Ferratomic.Concurrency passes.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-verify/lean/Ferratomic/Concurrency.lean
**Depends on**: None.
close_reason: Checkpoint model uses Datom.toTuple/ofTuple through intermediate tuple repr. Roundtrip proven via funext + image_image + image_id

title: DEFECT-002: INV-FERR-045 chunk_addr not collision-resistant, only forward direction
close_reason: Superseded by fix task bd-sam

title: Fix Kani harnesses using non-schema attributes (CR-017)
description:
**What**: snapshot_isolation, write_linearizability, transaction_atomicity use Attribute::from("test/counter") and "test/name" — not in genesis schema. With schema validation now implemented (bd-85j.11 closed), .commit(store.schema()) returns Err(UnknownAttribute) for these, making harnesses vacuous.
**Why**: INV-FERR-006, INV-FERR-007, INV-FERR-020. Harnesses silently pass via error path.
**Acceptance**:
1. Harnesses use genesis-schema attributes: db/doc (String), db/ident (Keyword), tx/provenance (String), or extend schema before testing.
2. All harnesses exercise the intended property, not the error path.
3. Harnesses compile and the properties they test are actually exercised.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-verify/kani/store_views.rs, ferratomic-verify/kani/durability.rs
**Depends on**: bd-85j.6 (Types — closed).

title: Implement Checkpoint: serialize Store to durable file (Phase 4a item 6)
description:
**What**: Serialize Store to checkpoint file with BLAKE3 integrity hash. Enables three-level recovery (checkpoint + WAL delta) instead of full WAL replay.
**Why**: INV-FERR-013 (checkpoint equivalence), INV-FERR-014 (recovery correctness). Session-001-bootstrap Phase 4a item 6. Without checkpoint, recovery is O(transactions) not O(datoms).
**Acceptance**:
1. checkpoint(store, path) serializes: magic + version + epoch + schema + datoms (EAVT-sorted) + BLAKE3 checksum.
2. load_checkpoint(path) deserializes and verifies BLAKE3 checksum. Returns Err on corruption.
3. Round-trip: load_checkpoint(checkpoint(store)) == store (datom set, epoch, schema).
4. Corrupted checkpoint rejected with CheckpointCorrupted error.
5. Database::recover() loads checkpoint then replays WAL delta (entries after checkpoint epoch).
6. Regression test: checkpoint → corrupt 1 byte → load fails.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/checkpoint.rs, ferratomic-core/src/storage.rs
**Depends on**: bd-85j.9 (WAL, for integrated recovery).

title: Rewrite 4 Kani CRDT harnesses to use Store, not BTreeSet (CR-011..014)
description:
**What**: Rewrite Kani harnesses that prove BTreeSet stdlib properties instead of ferratomic properties. Covers 7 harnesses across 3 files:
- crdt_laws.rs: merge_commutativity, merge_associativity, merge_idempotency, monotonic_growth (4 harnesses)
- durability.rs: recovery_superset, append_only (2 harnesses)
- store_views.rs: observer_monotonicity (1 harness — assumes its own conclusion via kani::assume)
Also: apply inv_ferr_NNN naming convention and INV-FERR assertion messages to all rewritten harnesses (subsumes bd-euw).
**Why**: INV-FERR-001..004, INV-FERR-014, INV-FERR-018, INV-FERR-011. These harnesses verify BTreeSet::union/insert properties, not ferratomic code. They give false confidence.
**Acceptance**:
1. All 7 harnesses use Store::from_datoms() and merge::merge() (or equivalent ferratomic APIs), not raw BTreeSet operations.
2. observer_monotonicity exercises real Observer::observe(), not kani::assume(next >= last).
3. recovery_superset exercises real Wal::recover() path.
4. All harness names prefixed with inv_ferr_NNN_.
5. All assertions include INV-FERR-NNN in failure message.
6. kani proofs pass (or are documented as expected-fail pending implementation).
**File(s)**: ferratomic-verify/kani/crdt_laws.rs, ferratomic-verify/kani/durability.rs, ferratomic-verify/kani/store_views.rs
**Depends on**: bd-85j.7 (Store must exist).

title: Migrate Store from BTreeSet to im::OrdSet per ADR-FERR-001
description:
**What**: Replace BTreeSet<Datom> with im::OrdSet<Datom> in Store. ADR-FERR-001 chose im-rs for O(1) snapshot cloning via structural sharing. Current BTreeSet requires O(n) clone for every snapshot.
**Why**: ADR-FERR-001 (spec/04-decisions-and-constraints.md). Session-001-bootstrap Phase 4a item 1 says "im::OrdMap indexes." AGENTS.md says "Implementation uses im::OrdSet/im::OrdMap." Current BTreeSet is a spec deviation.
**Acceptance**:
1. Store.datoms is im::OrdSet<Datom>, not BTreeSet<Datom>.
2. All 4 secondary indexes use im::OrdSet<Datom>.
3. Store::clone() is O(1) via structural sharing.
4. Store::datom_set() returns &im::OrdSet<Datom> (or compatible adapter).
5. All 69 existing tests pass.
6. from_datoms accepts BTreeSet for generator compatibility (converts internally).
7. cargo clippy clean, 0 warnings.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/store.rs, ferratomic-core/src/merge.rs
**Depends on**: None (Store already exists).
close_reason: Migrated Store from BTreeSet to im::OrdSet per ADR-FERR-001. O(1) snapshot via structural sharing. from_datoms still accepts BTreeSet for generator compat. from_merge uses OrdSet::union. All 69 tests pass, clippy clean.

title: Fix INV-FERR-053 light_client_soundness: replace tautology with axiom
description:
**What**: Replace vacuous light_client_structural_soundness (proves p.root=p.root) with axiomatized soundness connecting verify_inclusion to store membership.
**Why**: INV-FERR-053 (light client protocol). Current theorem is rfl tautology — hypothesis unused.
**Acceptance**:
1. Add axiom store_root : DatomStore → CryptoHash.
2. Add axiom datom_in_proof : InclusionProof → Datom → Prop.
3. Replace theorem with axiom light_client_soundness linking verify_inclusion success + root match to store membership.
4. lake build Ferratomic.VKN passes, zero sorry.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-verify/lean/Ferratomic/VKN.lean
**Depends on**: None.
close_reason: light_client_soundness replaced with axiom linking verify_inclusion to store membership

title: DEFECT-009: INV-FERR-039 missing selective_merge_idemp theorem
close_reason: Superseded by fix task bd-2d7

title: Fix Kani write_linearizability silent failure swallowing (CR-018)
description:
**What**: Fix assertion gaps in 2 Kani files:
- store_views.rs: write_linearizability silently swallows transact failures (if let Ok → empty epochs → vacuous loop).
- schema_identity.rs: content_identity creates different-content EntityId but never asserts inequality (CR-029).
**Why**: INV-FERR-007, INV-FERR-012. A Store that rejects everything passes write_linearizability. Missing collision-resistance assertion.
**Acceptance**:
1. write_linearizability asserts at least one transaction succeeds: assert!(!epochs.is_empty()).
2. content_identity asserts id1 != id3 for different content (remove _ prefix on id3).
**File(s)**: ferratomic-verify/kani/store_views.rs, ferratomic-verify/kani/schema_identity.rs
**Depends on**: bd-85j.6 (Types must exist for compilation).

title: BUG: from_merge schema union is asymmetric on conflicting attributes (DEFECT-II-001)
description:
**Observed**: from_merge uses if !schema.contains(attr) to skip B's definition when A already defines the attribute. If A defines user/age as Long and B defines user/age as String, merge(A,B).schema has Long but merge(B,A).schema has String. Schema-level commutativity violated.
**Expected**: INV-FERR-043 (schema compatibility): shared attributes must have identical type+cardinality. INV-FERR-001: merge must be commutative at every level including schema.
**Reproducer**: merge two stores where both define same attribute with different ValueType — schema differs depending on argument order.
**Root cause**: from_merge uses first-writer-wins for schema conflicts instead of symmetric resolution.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-001, INV-FERR-043
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/store.rs
**Fix**: Replace if !schema.contains with deterministic symmetric resolution: when both define the same attribute, assert definitions are equal (debug_assert), or pick the definition deterministically (e.g., the one with the lexicographically smaller ValueType debug string). Add test for conflicting and disjoint schema merges.
close_reason: Fixed: from_merge schema union now symmetric. Iterates both schemas, detects conflicts with debug_assert, uses deterministic Debug-sort resolution. INV-FERR-001, INV-FERR-043.

title: DEFECT-001: INV-FERR-053 light_client_soundness proves tautology (rfl)
close_reason: Superseded by fix task bd-2qe

title: BUG: Database.transact() does not write WAL before epoch advance (DEFECT-III-001)
description:
**Observed**: Database::transact() calls new_store.transact() then ArcSwap::store() without any WAL involvement. The WAL module exists but is not wired into the Database transact path. visible(SNAP(e)) happens without durable(WAL(T)).
**Expected**: INV-FERR-008: durable(WAL(T)) BEFORE visible(SNAP(e)). Database should append to WAL, fsync, then apply to store and swap pointer.
**Reproducer**: Database::genesis().transact(tx) succeeds but no WAL file is written.
**Root cause**: WAL and Database were implemented by separate agents. The wiring between them was not done.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-008
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/db.rs
**Fix**: Database holds Option<Wal>. transact() calls wal.append(epoch, &tx) + wal.fsync() before new_store.transact(). Add Database::genesis_with_wal(path) and Database::recover_from_wal(path) constructors. Regression test: write via Database, recover WAL, verify datoms match.
close_reason: Fixed: Database now holds Option<Wal>. transact() calls wal.append() + wal.fsync() BEFORE store.transact(). Added genesis_with_wal() and recover_from_wal(). 2 regression tests pass. INV-FERR-008.

title: BUG: TxValidationError not convertible to FerraError (DEFECT-I-011)
description:
**Observed**: Transaction::commit() returns Result<_, TxValidationError> while Store::transact() returns Result<_, FerraError>. Callers of commit → transact must handle two different error types. FerraError already has UnknownAttribute and SchemaViolation variants.
**Expected**: INV-FERR-019: all errors through FerraError. Or at minimum From<TxValidationError> for FerraError.
**Reproducer**: tx.commit(schema).and_then(|t| store.transact(t)) — types don't unify.
**Root cause**: Error types designed independently by different agents.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-019 (error exhaustiveness)
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/writer.rs, ferratom/src/error.rs
**Fix**: Add impl From<TxValidationError> for FerraError mapping UnknownAttribute → FerraError::UnknownAttribute, SchemaViolation → FerraError::SchemaViolation.
close_reason: Fixed: impl From<TxValidationError> for FerraError added to writer.rs. All 3 variants mapped. INV-FERR-019.

title: DEFECT-005: INV-FERR-010 merge_convergence is trivial alias for merge_comm
close_reason: Superseded by fix task bd-sy4

title: DEFECT-006: INV-FERR-051 missing key-binding theorem (wrong-key rejection)
close_reason: Superseded by fix task bd-23g

title: BUG: value_matches_type test skips Double variant (DEFECT-I-008)
description:
**Observed**: test_value_matches_type_all_variants explicitly omits Value::Double with a comment about OrderedFloat dependency. But ferratom re-exports Value::Double(OrderedFloat<f64>) and ferratomic-core transitively has access.
**Expected**: INV-FERR-009: all 11 value variants tested for type matching.
**Reproducer**: Read writer.rs test at line 476 — explicit skip comment.
**Root cause**: Author incorrectly believed ordered-float was not available in ferratomic-core's test scope.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-009 (test completeness)
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/writer.rs
**Fix**: Add (Value::Double(ordered_float::OrderedFloat(1.0)), ValueType::Double) to the pairs vec. ordered_float is available transitively through ferratom.
close_reason: Fixed: Value::Double(OrderedFloat(1.0)) added to test_value_matches_type_all_variants. All 11 variants now tested. ordered-float as dev-dep.

title: BUG: WAL tests don't verify payload content roundtrip (DEFECT-III-002)
description:
**Observed**: test_inv_ferr_008_create_append_recover verifies entry count and epoch but never deserializes payload to verify datom content survives roundtrip.
**Expected**: INV-FERR-008. Deserialize entries[0].payload back to Vec<Datom> and verify field values match what was written.
**Reproducer**: A WAL that serializes wrong field values would pass current tests.
**Root cause**: Test written for structure, not content.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-008
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/wal.rs
**Fix**: Add serde_json::from_slice::<Vec<Datom>>(&entries[0].payload) assertion checking attribute == "db/doc".
close_reason: Fixed: test_bug_bd_32t_payload_content_roundtrip deserializes WAL payload to Vec<Datom> and verifies attribute survives roundtrip. INV-FERR-008.

title: DEFECT-004: INV-FERR-046 history independence proof vacuous (Finset tautology)
close_reason: Superseded by fix task bd-sam

title: BUG: test_snapshot_is_frozen checks count not content (DEFECT-I-010)
description:
**Observed**: test_snapshot_is_frozen compares snap.datoms().count() before/after insert. A snapshot that swapped one datom for another (keeping count) would pass.
**Expected**: INV-FERR-006: compare datom SETS, not counts.
**Reproducer**: Read store.rs test at line 656 — count-based comparison.
**Root cause**: Weak assertion pattern.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-006 (test adequacy)
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/store.rs
**Fix**: Collect into BTreeSet and compare set equality.
close_reason: Fixed: test_snapshot_is_frozen now compares BTreeSet of datoms, not count. INV-FERR-006.

title: BUG: Store::snapshot() clones entire BTreeSet — O(n) not O(1) (DEFECT-I-005)
description:
**Observed**: snapshot() does Arc::new(self.datoms.clone()) — full O(n) clone of the BTreeSet.
**Expected**: INV-FERR-006 + ADR-FERR-001: O(1) snapshot via structural sharing (im::OrdMap) or ArcSwap.
**Reproducer**: At 100K datoms, snapshot() takes measurable time proportional to store size.
**Root cause**: Phase 4a-MVP uses BTreeSet instead of im::OrdSet. Known limitation.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-006 (performance aspect), INV-FERR-027 (read P99.99)
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/store.rs
**Fix**: Deferred to bd-85j.8 (MVCC with ArcSwap) or migration to im::OrdSet. Add TODO(Phase 4b) comment.
**Depends on**: bd-85j.8
close_reason: Fixed by bd-2pf: Store uses im::OrdSet. snapshot() is O(1) structural sharing. No Arc needed.

title: DEFECT-010: INV-FERR-013 checkpoint model unfalsifiable (identity functions)
close_reason: Superseded by fix task bd-2hs

title: Fix documentation inconsistencies from cleanroom audit (CR-021..024, CR-032..033)
description:
**What**: Fix 6 documentation defects plus add Lean model fidelity documentation. Spec becomes self-consistent and the Lean-Rust bridge gap is explicitly documented.
**Why**: Agents reading spec/README.md, AGENTS.md, QUICKSTART.md get wrong invariant counts, missing constraint definitions, and confusing phase messaging. Lean model gap undocumented per ADR-FERR-007.
**Acceptance**:
1. spec/README.md and AGENTS.md both say "55 invariants, 9 ADRs, 5 negative cases" (CR-021).
2. spec/README.md module table includes INV-FERR-051..055 in federation row or separate VKN row (CR-022).
3. spec/00-preamble.md has traceability note explaining SEED.md and 01-store.md are external documents, with key axioms (L1-L5) and constraints (C1-C8) defined inline (CR-023, CR-024).
4. QUICKSTART.md line 9 reworded: "Phase 3 will create stubs to make tests compilable" (CR-032).
5. spec/00-preamble.md §23.0.2 clarifies "zero project-internal dependencies" (CR-033).
6. spec/00-preamble.md §23.0.4 has "Model Fidelity" subsection listing abstracted aspects (hashing, serialization, epoch ordering, crypto) and which verification layer covers each instead (merged from bd-1bc).
**File(s)**: spec/README.md, spec/00-preamble.md, AGENTS.md, QUICKSTART.md
**Depends on**: None.
close_reason: All 6 acceptance criteria met: invariant counts consistent (55/9/5), VKN row in module table, traceability note + constraints defined in preamble, QUICKSTART reworded, zero-deps clarified, model fidelity documented.

title: Update Phase 2 integration tests to use Database API for WAL-backed operations
description:
**What**: Integration tests in test_recovery.rs test 3 call Store::genesis_with_wal and Store::recover_from_wal. These methods exist on Database, not Store. Also: test_snapshot.rs tests 3-4 use Attribute::from("test/counter") which is not in genesis schema. Update all integration tests to compile against the actual API.
**Why**: Phase 4a acceptance gate: "ALL Phase 2 tests pass." Tests cannot even compile until API references match the implementation.
**Acceptance**:
1. test_recovery.rs test 3 uses Database::genesis_with_wal and Database::recover_from_wal.
2. test_snapshot.rs tests 3-4 use genesis-schema attributes (db/doc for String, db/ident for Keyword).
3. All 4 integration test files compile (may fail — that's expected until full Phase 4a is wired).
4. test_schema.rs all 5 tests pass against current implementation.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-verify/integration/test_recovery.rs, ferratomic-verify/integration/test_snapshot.rs
**Depends on**: None (the API exists, tests just reference the wrong types).

title: Fix partition_safe_write Lean tautology (CR-002)
description:
**What**: partition_safe_write proves ∃ s', s' = apply_tx s d — trivially true for any total function. Lean cannot meaningfully model partitions.
**Why**: INV-FERR-035. Theorem proves function totality, not partition safety.
**Preferred fix**: Remove V:LEAN tag from INV-FERR-035 in spec/04-decisions-and-constraints.md. Rely on V:MODEL (Stateright) which CAN model partitions. Delete partition_safe_write theorem.
**Alternative**: Model partitions in Lean (disproportionate effort for Stage 0 invariant).
**Acceptance**:
1. V:LEAN removed from INV-FERR-035 verification tags in spec.
2. partition_safe_write deleted from Decisions.lean.
3. partition_recovery, partition_recovery_order, partition_recovery_idempotent retained (they prove useful merge properties).
4. lake build succeeds. Spec updated.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-verify/lean/Ferratomic/Decisions.lean, spec/04-decisions-and-constraints.md
**Depends on**: None.

title: BUG: from_merge not tested for disjoint or conflicting schemas (DEFECT-II-003)
description:
**Observed**: test_bug_bd_10p_merge_preserves_schema only tests merging two identical genesis stores. Never tests merging stores with different or conflicting attribute definitions.
**Expected**: INV-FERR-009, INV-FERR-043. Tests should cover: (1) disjoint schemas produce union of all attributes, (2) conflicting definitions are handled deterministically.
**Reproducer**: No test exists for schema merge with disjoint or conflicting attributes.
**Root cause**: Regression test was minimal — validated fix but not edge cases.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-009, INV-FERR-043
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/store.rs
**Fix**: Add test_merge_disjoint_schemas and test_merge_conflicting_schemas. Depends on DEFECT-II-001 fix for correct behavior on conflicts.
close_reason: Fixed: test_bug_bd_3n6_merge_disjoint_schemas and test_bug_bd_3n6_merge_schema_commutativity added. Disjoint schema union verified (6 attrs from 5+5 with 4 overlap). Commutativity verified. Both pass.

title: DEFECT-003: INV-FERR-049 root_hash modeled as .card, not content-sensitive
close_reason: Superseded by fix task bd-sam

title: Fix test generators: Op::Assert bias and attribute regex (CR-025, CR-026)
description:
**What**: arb_schema_valid_datom and arb_datom_with_unknown_attr hardcode Op::Assert. arb_attribute regex [a-z][a-z0-9_]*/... cannot produce camelCase (db/valueType). Schema validation of retractions and camelCase attributes never tested.
**Why**: INV-FERR-009. Systematic blind spot in property-based testing.
**Acceptance**:
1. arb_schema_valid_datom and arb_datom_with_unknown_attr generate both Op::Assert and Op::Retract.
2. arb_attribute regex covers camelCase (db/valueType pattern).
3. Existing proptest properties still compile (will fail until Phase 4).
**File(s)**: ferratomic-verify/src/generators.rs
**Depends on**: bd-85j.6 (Types must exist for generators to compile).

title: BUG: Store::genesis_with_wal and recover_from_wal not implemented (DEFECT-III-003)
description:
**Observed**: Integration test inv_ferr_008_wal_entry_precedes_snapshot calls Store::genesis_with_wal(&path) and Store::recover_from_wal(&path). Neither method exists. Test cannot compile.
**Expected**: INV-FERR-008, INV-FERR-014. Either (a) implement on Store, or (b) wire WAL into Database and update test to use Database instead.
**Reproducer**: Attempt to compile ferratomic-verify integration tests — fails on missing methods.
**Root cause**: WAL-Store integration not yet wired. Test was written before implementation.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-008, INV-FERR-014
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/db.rs, ferratomic-verify/integration/test_recovery.rs
**Fix**: Implement Database::genesis_with_wal(path) + Database::recover_from_wal(path) per DEFECT-III-001 fix. Update test_recovery.rs test 3 to use Database.
**Depends on**: DEFECT-III-001 (WAL wiring).
close_reason: Fixed by bd-2w9: Database::genesis_with_wal() and Database::recover_from_wal() implemented. Integration test_recovery.rs test 3 needs updating to use Database instead of Store (tracked separately if needed). INV-FERR-008, INV-FERR-014.

title: BUG: Missing test for MERGE exempt from schema validation (C4)
description: No test verified merge accepts datoms with unknown attributes. Spec: MERGE exempt from schema (C4). FIXED: added proptest+integration tests. Affected: INV-FERR-009, C4
close_reason: Fixed: added inv_ferr_009_merge_exempt_from_schema in proptest + integration

title: BUG: Transaction has no retract_datom method (DEFECT-I-006)
description:
**Observed**: Only assert_datom() exists on Transaction<Building>. No way to create retraction transactions.
**Expected**: INV-FERR-018: Retractions are new datoms with Op::Retract. INV-FERR-029: LIVE view depends on assert/retract pairs. Without retract_datom, no test or production code can create retractions.
**Reproducer**: Attempt to build a retraction transaction — no method exists.
**Root cause**: Omission during initial implementation. Only the assert path was built.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-018, INV-FERR-029, INV-FERR-032
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/writer.rs
**Fix**: Add retract_datom(self, entity, attribute, value) -> Self mirroring assert_datom but with Op::Retract.
close_reason: Fixed: retract_datom() added to Transaction<Building>. Mirrors assert_datom with Op::Retract. INV-FERR-018, INV-FERR-029.

title: EPIC: Ferratomic — Formally verified distributed embedded datom database engine
FERR-P1: Lean 4 proofs for all INV-FERR with V:LEAN tag
close_reason: 78 Lean theorems, 28 INV-FERR, zero sorry — pushed to main

title: FERR-P4A-OBSERVER: DatomObserver trait + broadcast
description:
**What**: Complete the DatomObserver system: broadcast channel + catch-up protocol.
**Why**: INV-FERR-011 (observer epoch monotonicity), ADR-FERR-004 (at-least-once delivery).

**DONE** (implemented in observer.rs):
- Observer struct with AtomicU64 monotonic epoch tracking
- Observer::new(agent), observe(&store) → Snapshot, last_epoch()

**REMAINING**:
- DatomObserver trait: on_commit(epoch, &[Datom]), on_catchup(from_epoch, &[Datom]), name()
- Bounded broadcast ring buffer (default 1024 entries)
- Catch-up protocol for slow/restarted observers
- Wire into Database.transact() to notify registered observers after epoch advance

**Acceptance**:
1. DatomObserver trait defined with on_commit, on_catchup, name methods.
2. Database::register_observer(Box<dyn DatomObserver>) stores observer.
3. After transact(), all registered observers receive on_commit with new datoms.
4. Broadcast channel is bounded (ring buffer, configurable capacity).
5. Slow observer that missed entries receives on_catchup with delta datoms.
6. integration test_snapshot.rs observer tests pass.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/observer.rs, ferratomic-core/src/db.rs
**Depends on**: bd-85j.8 (MVCC — closed).

title: FERR-P4A-SCHEMA: Schema validation + genesis bootstrap
description:
**What**: Schema validation at transact boundary + deterministic genesis bootstrap.
**Why**: INV-FERR-009 (schema validation), INV-FERR-031 (genesis determinism).
**Acceptance**:
1. Transaction::commit(schema) validates all datoms against schema. Rejects atomically on any failure.
2. Schema-as-data: schema evolution via transactions (new attributes defined as datoms).
3. genesis() produces deterministic Store with 19 axiomatic meta-schema attributes.
4. genesis() hash is identical across all invocations.
5. MERGE exempt from schema validation (C4: merge is pure set union).
6. proptest schema_properties.rs tests pass.
7. integration test_schema.rs tests pass.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/store.rs (schema integration), ferratom/src/schema.rs (types)
**Depends on**: bd-85j.7 (Store).
close_reason: 19-attribute genesis schema + tx metadata datoms in transact(). Schema evolution via evolve_schema(). commit() validates against schema. All 73 tests pass, clippy clean. INV-FERR-009, INV-FERR-031, INV-FERR-004.

title: FERR-P4B-BENCH: Scaling benchmarks (1K-100M datoms)
FERR-P4B-PROLLY: Prolly tree block store
FERR-P4B-SHARD: Entity-hash sharding
FERR-P4C-TRANSPORT: Transport trait + implementations
FERR-P4C-FEDERATION: CRDT mesh federation
FERR-P4D-DATALOG: Query engine (parser, planner, evaluator)
FERR-P5-MIGRATE: braid-kernel migration to ferratomic
FERR-P2-STATERIGHT: Stateright model checking
close_reason: Phase 2 Stateright complete for MVP scope. Open sub-bugs (bd-85j.2.3, bd-85j.2.3.1) reparented as standalone.

title: BUG: canonical_nodes helper triggers dead-code warning in non-test builds
description:
**Observed**: canonical_nodes is only used by tests but is compiled in non-test builds, producing an unused warning.
**Expected**: The Stateright module should stay warning-free under the project's -D warnings quality gate.
**Reproducer**: Building the module through a scratch crate emits 'associated function canonical_nodes is never used'.
**Root cause**: Test-only helper remained in the main impl block.
**Affected INV**: None (quality gate defect)

title: BUG: stateright module docs still describe pre-wiring state
description:
**Observed**: ferratomic-verify/stateright/mod.rs says the subtree is isolated from crate wiring work that will import it later, but ferratomic-verify/src/lib.rs already imports it as stateright_models.
**Expected**: Module docs should describe the current wired state.
**Reproducer**: Compare ferratomic-verify/stateright/mod.rs doc comment with ferratomic-verify/src/lib.rs.
**Root cause**: Documentation was not updated after the wiring change.
**Affected INV**: None (documentation defect)

title: BUG: CrdtModel convergence property is existential instead of invariant
description:
**Observed**: The Stateright model uses Property::sometimes for convergence and the test only asserts that one converged path exists.
**Expected**: INV-FERR-010 requires an invariant-style convergence check over quiescent states, matching the spec's Stateright contract.
**Reproducer**: ferratomic-verify/stateright/crdt_model.rs: inv_ferr_010_convergence uses Property::sometimes and inv_ferr_010_model_checker_finds_a_converged_state uses assert_any_discovery.
**Root cause**: The model encoded convergence as existential reachability instead of a safety property.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-010
notes: Blocked pending spec clarification in bd-85j.2.3.1; current state machine reaches quiescent divergent states after local writes.

title: SPEC: Clarify INV-FERR-010 Stateright contract for SEC vs eventual dissemination
description:
**Observed**: The Level 0/1 SEC wording for INV-FERR-010 is conditional on replicas having the same update set, but the Level 2 Stateright snippet encodes an always-property over quiescent states that rejects reachable states after a single local write.
**Expected**: The spec should choose one precise Stateright contract: either SEC under equal update sets, or eventual full dissemination under additional fairness/history assumptions.
**Reproducer**: In the current state machine, a local Write action yields a quiescent state with differing node sets and no in-flight messages.
**Root cause**: The model property mixes a conditional SEC statement with a stronger quiescent-equality check.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-010

title: BUG: CrdtModel prunes duplicate merge messages from the protocol state space
description:
**Observed**: can_queue_snapshot rejects redundant/subset payloads and identical pending merge messages, so the model cannot explore replay/no-op merge schedules.
**Expected**: INV-FERR-003 and INV-FERR-010 should be verified against redundant merge delivery, not optimized out of the model.
**Reproducer**: From a state with Write(0, Datom::from_seed(0)) and InitMerge(0,1), a second InitMerge(0,1) returns None in the current state machine.
**Root cause**: State-space reduction logic changed the protocol semantics by suppressing legal duplicate snapshots.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-003, INV-FERR-010
close_reason: Fixed: duplicate merge snapshots remain reachable and regression test test_bug_bd_85j_2_4_duplicate_merge_messages_are_modeled passes in isolated Stateright validation

title: FERR-P2-KANI: Kani bounded model checking
close_reason: Phase 2 Kani complete for MVP scope. Open sub-bugs (bd-8e3, bd-1kh) reparented as standalone.

title: FERR-P2-PROPTEST: Property-based test suite
close_reason: Red-phase proptest suite committed: generators.rs + crdt_properties.rs (INV-FERR-001..004, 010, 012) + index_properties.rs (005-007) + wal_properties.rs (008) + schema_properties.rs (009, 011)

title: FERR-P2-INTEGRATION: Integration tests (ALL MUST FAIL initially)
close_reason: Red-phase integration tests committed: test_crdt.rs (001-004, 010, 012) + test_snapshot.rs (006, 007, 011) + test_recovery.rs (008) + test_schema.rs (009, 031)

title: FERR-P3-TYPES: ferratom crate type definitions
description:
**What**: Implement all ferratom crate types. The workspace compiles. Phase 2 tests compile-and-fail (red phase complete).
**Why**: bd-85j.6 is the critical path bottleneck (100% betweenness centrality). Unblocks all Phase 4a work (bd-85j.7..11) and 4 audit remediation tasks. Types encode invariants at the type level per Curry-Howard correspondence.
**Acceptance**:
1. Datom: 5-field struct with Eq/Hash/Ord on all fields. Immutable (no &mut methods). INV-FERR-012, INV-FERR-018.
2. EntityId: [u8; 32] newtype. Private inner. Only constructible via EntityId::from_content(&[u8]) which BLAKE3-hashes. EntityId::from_bytes(&[u8; 32]) for testing only. INV-FERR-012.
3. Attribute: Arc<str> newtype. From<&str>. O(1) clone. INV-FERR-026.
4. Value: 11-variant enum (Keyword, String, Long, Double, Boolean, Instant, Uuid, Bytes, Ref, BigInt, BigDec) with Arc sharing for String/Bytes. INV-FERR-009.
5. Op: Assert | Retract, Copy. INV-FERR-018.
6. TxId: (physical: u64, logical: u16, agent: AgentId), Ord via lexicographic comparison. INV-FERR-015.
7. AgentId: [u8; 16], Copy, Ord via lexicographic byte comparison. 
8. HybridClock: tick() monotonic, receive() causal. INV-FERR-015, INV-FERR-016.
9. Frontier: HashMap<AgentId, TxId>. INV-FERR-016.
10. Schema: im::HashMap<Attribute, AttributeDef>. INV-FERR-009.
11. AttributeDef: ValueType, Cardinality, ResolutionMode (Lww + MultiValue only for Phase 4a). INV-FERR-009, INV-FERR-032.
12. FerraError: exhaustive enum with 5 categories (Storage, Validation, Concurrency, Federation, InvariantViolation). INV-FERR-019, NEG-FERR-001.
13. Semilattice trait: merge(&self, &Self) -> Self with comm+assoc+idemp. INV-FERR-001..003.
14. ContentAddressed trait: content_hash(&self) -> [u8; 32]. INV-FERR-012.
15. cargo check -p ferratom passes.
16. cargo clippy -p ferratom -- -D warnings zero warnings.
17. #![forbid(unsafe_code)], #![deny(missing_docs)] in lib.rs.
18. ferratomic-verify generators (generators.rs) compile against real types.
**File(s)**: ferratom/src/datom.rs, ferratom/src/clock.rs, ferratom/src/schema.rs, ferratom/src/error.rs, ferratom/src/traits.rs, ferratom/src/lib.rs
**Depends on**: bd-85j.1 (Lean proofs), bd-85j.4 (proptest), bd-85j.5 (integration) — all closed.
close_reason: Phase 3 types complete. 14 types across 6 files (1385 LOC). All quality gates pass: check clean, clippy 0 warnings, fmt clean, 37 tests pass. Generators compile target types exist.

title: FERR-P4A-STORE: Store with im::OrdMap indexes
description:
**What**: Implement Store struct with im::OrdMap persistent indexes. The core data structure.
**Why**: INV-FERR-001..005, INV-FERR-018. Store is the semilattice: (P(D), ∪).
**Acceptance**:
1. Store holds datoms in im::OrdSet<Datom> primary set.
2. 5 secondary indexes (EAVT, entity, attribute, VAET, AVET) as im::OrdMap, rebuilt on every mutation.
3. Store::from_datoms(datoms) constructs store + indexes.
4. Store::transact(tx) applies datoms, returns TxReceipt. Strict growth (INV-FERR-004).
5. merge::merge(&Store, &Store) returns set union. Commutative, associative, idempotent.
6. verify_index_bijection(&Store) returns true for all reachable states (INV-FERR-005).
7. Store is Clone (O(1) via im-rs structural sharing).
8. proptest crdt_properties.rs tests pass.
9. integration test_crdt.rs tests pass.
10. cargo clippy clean, #![forbid(unsafe_code)].
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/store.rs, ferratomic-core/src/merge.rs
**Depends on**: bd-85j.6 (Types).
close_reason: Store + merge + Transaction types implemented. 1249 LOC across 3 files. All quality gates pass: check clean, clippy 0 warnings, 24 tests pass. Store with BTreeSet primary, 4 secondary indexes (INV-FERR-005 bijection), genesis schema (INV-FERR-031), schema evolution, transact with epoch tracking (INV-FERR-004, INV-FERR-007).

title: FERR-P4A-MVCC: ArcSwap snapshots + WriterActor
description:
**What**: MVCC snapshot isolation via ArcSwap + single WriterActor.
**Why**: INV-FERR-006 (snapshot isolation), INV-FERR-007 (write linearizability).
**Acceptance**:
1. Database wraps Store in ArcSwap. Readers call db.snapshot() → lock-free Arc load.
2. WriterActor serializes writes via mpsc channel. Group commit batches pending transactions.
3. Snapshots are immutable: concurrent writes don't affect existing snapshots.
4. Epochs strictly increase per committed transaction.
5. proptest index_properties.rs tests pass.
6. integration test_snapshot.rs tests pass.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/db.rs, ferratomic-core/src/snapshot.rs, ferratomic-core/src/writer.rs
**Depends on**: bd-85j.7 (Store).
close_reason: Database (ArcSwap + write Mutex) and Observer (AtomicU64 monotonic epoch) implemented. 73 total tests pass, clippy clean. INV-FERR-006 (snapshot isolation), INV-FERR-007 (write linearizability), INV-FERR-011 (observer monotonicity). Integration test acceptance deferred pending schema expansion (bd-85j.11) — tests use non-genesis attributes.

title: FERR-P4A-WAL: Write-ahead log with group commit
description:
**What**: Write-ahead log with two-fsync barrier and crash recovery.
**Why**: INV-FERR-008 (WAL fsync ordering), INV-FERR-014 (recovery correctness).
**Acceptance**:
1. Wal::append(epoch, tx) writes frame: magic + version + epoch + length + payload + CRC32.
2. Wal::fsync() calls sync_all() and updates last_synced_epoch.
3. WAL fsync completes BEFORE epoch advance (two-fsync barrier).
4. Wal::recover() deserializes complete entries, truncates incomplete.
5. proptest wal_properties.rs tests pass.
6. integration test_recovery.rs tests pass.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/wal.rs, ferratomic-core/src/checkpoint.rs, ferratomic-core/src/storage.rs
**Depends on**: bd-85j.7 (Store).
close_reason: WAL implemented: create/open, append with FERR magic + CRC32, fsync, recover with truncation. 10 new tests. 83 total pass, clippy clean. INV-FERR-008, INV-FERR-014.

title: DEFECT-008: INV-FERR-029 missing live_bounded theorem
close_reason: Superseded by fix task bd-200

title: BUG: Kani harnesses use non-canonical verification APIs
description:
**Observed**: `ferratomic-verify/kani/*.rs` imports `store::{Committed, Transaction}` and uses raw datom/store fields, while the rest of the verification crate uses `writer::Transaction`, accessor methods, and store projection helpers.
**Expected**: The verification crate should expose one consistent intended API surface so red-phase proof modules converge on the same contracts.
**Reproducer**: Static comparison of `ferratomic-verify/kani/*.rs` against `ferratomic-verify/src/generators.rs`, `ferratomic-verify/proptest/*.rs`, and `ferratomic-verify/integration/*.rs`.
**Root cause**: Kani harnesses were drafted directly from spec sketches instead of being aligned to the verification crate's existing helper surface.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-005, INV-FERR-006, INV-FERR-009, INV-FERR-017, INV-FERR-020
close_reason: Superseded by audit beads bd-2p4 (BTreeSet tautologies), bd-2sr (assertion gaps), bd-2jt (schema attrs). Non-canonical API concerns addressed in full Kani rewrite.

title: BUG: AttributeDef has pub fields bypassing constructor (DEFECT-I-009)
description:
**Observed**: All 4 fields of AttributeDef are pub. Allows direct struct construction bypassing any future validation.
**Expected**: AGENTS.md type discipline: "No pub fields that bypass constructors."
**Reproducer**: AttributeDef { value_type: ..., cardinality: ..., ... } compiles without constructor.
**Root cause**: Simple struct with no current invariants — pub fields were pragmatic for Phase 4a.
**Affected INV**: Type discipline (not a named INV-FERR)
**File(s)**: ferratom/src/schema.rs
**Fix**: When resolution modes expand beyond Lww/MultiValue (Phase 4b+), make fields private and add a builder/constructor. Acceptable as-is for Phase 4a.

title: BUG: Kani harness names do not cross-reference INV-FERR coverage
description:
**Observed**: Kani proofs are named `merge_commutativity`, `index_bijection`, `checkpoint_roundtrip`, etc., and several assertions omit invariant-tagged failure messages.
**Expected**: Verification names and failures should be grep-able by invariant, using the `inv_ferr_NNN_*` convention and `INV-FERR-NNN` assertion messages.
**Reproducer**: Static inspection of `ferratomic-verify/kani/*.rs`.
**Root cause**: The Kani modules were added before being normalized to the project's test naming and failure-message conventions.
**Affected INV**: Coverage traceability across INV-FERR-001..020
close_reason: Folded into bd-2p4 (Kani CRDT rewrite). INV-FERR naming convention will be applied as part of the full harness rewrite.

title: BUG: Zero-variation generators for schema rejection tests
description: arb_datom_with_unknown_attr/arb_datom_with_wrong_type always produced same input. FIXED: randomized attrs + 4 mismatch pairs. Affected: INV-FERR-009
close_reason: Fixed: unknown-attr gen uses zztest/ prefix; wrong-type gen uses 4 distinct mismatch pairs

title: BUG: HybridClock::tick() saturating_add can produce duplicate TxId (DEFECT-I-004)
description:
**Observed**: When logical counter reaches u32::MAX and wall clock hasn't advanced, saturating_add(1) returns u32::MAX again. Two consecutive tick() calls return identical TxId, violating strict monotonicity.
**Expected**: INV-FERR-015: every tick() must produce a TxId strictly greater than the previous. Spec says "blocks until the physical clock advances" on overflow.
**Reproducer**: Set physical to current wall_clock, set logical to u32::MAX, call tick() twice — both return same TxId.
**Root cause**: saturating_add chosen for simplicity over correctness.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-015
**File(s)**: ferratom/src/clock.rs
**Fix**: Replace saturating_add with checked_add. On None, busy-wait loop until wall_clock() > self.physical, then reset logical to 0. Or return Result<TxId, FerraError> with backpressure error.
close_reason: Fixed: tick() now uses checked_add with busy-wait backpressure on logical overflow. Strict monotonicity guaranteed. INV-FERR-015.

title: Fix Stateright Datom::from_seed field correlation (CR-016)
description:
**What**: Datom::from_seed derives all 5 fields from single u64. Entity uniquely determines attribute, value, tx, op. Model cannot explore same entity with different attributes, assert+retract pairs, or multi-datom transactions.
**Why**: INV-FERR-010. State space systematically excludes many CRDT-relevant scenarios.
**Acceptance**:
1. Datom fields vary independently (at minimum: entity and op decoupled).
2. Model can represent: same entity with different attributes, assert+retract on same entity.
3. Existing Stateright tests still pass.
4. State space remains explorable (< 10M states with max_datoms=3, node_count=3). If independence causes explosion, reduce max_datoms or restrict value ranges to preserve bounded model checking.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-verify/stateright/crdt_model.rs
**Depends on**: None.

title: BUG: db.rs genesis doc comment says 4 attributes, should be 19 (DEFECT-III-004)
description:
**Observed**: db.rs line 49-51 doc comment says "The genesis store contains the 4 core meta-schema attributes." Genesis now has 19 per bd-85j.11.
**Expected**: INV-FERR-031. Documentation must match reality.
**Reproducer**: Read db.rs line 49.
**Root cause**: Doc not updated after genesis expansion.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-031 (documentation)
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/db.rs
**Fix**: Change "4 core meta-schema attributes" to "19 axiomatic meta-schema attributes."
close_reason: Fixed: doc comment updated from 4 to 19 axiomatic meta-schema attributes. INV-FERR-031.

title: Strengthen integration test assertions: content not count (CR-027, CR-028)
description:
**What**: test_recovery::wal_write_and_recover checks entry count, not content. test_snapshot::snapshot_stability checks datom count, not set equality. Both would pass with wrong data.
**Why**: INV-FERR-008, INV-FERR-006. Weak assertions miss data corruption.
**Acceptance**:
1. WAL recovery test asserts recovered entry contains expected datom content.
2. Snapshot stability test compares BTreeSet of datoms, not .count().
**File(s)**: ferratomic-verify/integration/test_recovery.rs, ferratomic-verify/integration/test_snapshot.rs
**Depends on**: bd-85j.6 (Types must exist).

title: Fix ProllyTree axiom model: collision-resistant hash + root hash + history independence
description:
**What**: Replace concrete Nat models in ProllyTree.lean with axiom-based abstract types. Fixes DEFECT-002, DEFECT-003, DEFECT-004.
**Why**: INV-FERR-045 (collision resistance), INV-FERR-046 (history independence), INV-FERR-049 (snapshot = root hash). Current chunk_addr uses addition (non-injective). root_hash uses .card (loses content). history_independence is tautology.
**Acceptance**:
1. axiom content_hash with collision resistance axiom replaces chunk_addr.
2. axiom prolly_root with injectivity axiom replaces root_hash.
3. history_independence follows from Finset + axiom signature.
4. chunk_store_idempotent still proven.
5. lake build Ferratomic.ProllyTree passes, zero sorry.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-verify/lean/Ferratomic/ProllyTree.lean
**Depends on**: None.
close_reason: ProllyTree axiom model: ContentHash + prolly_root with collision resistance + injectivity

title: Strengthen INV-FERR-010 convergence: permutation-invariant apply_tx foldl
description:
**What**: Prove foldl apply_tx ∅ ds = ds.toFinset, then derive convergence_perm for list permutations. Replace trivial merge_convergence alias.
**Why**: INV-FERR-010 Level 0 requires: same updates in any order → same state. Current merge_convergence is identical to merge_comm.
**Acceptance**:
1. theorem apply_tx_foldl_eq_toFinset via list induction.
2. theorem convergence_perm: ds₁ ~ ds₂ → foldl apply_tx ∅ ds₁ = foldl apply_tx ∅ ds₂.
3. import Mathlib.Data.List.Perm added.
4. lake build Ferratomic.Store passes.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-verify/lean/Ferratomic/Store.lean
**Depends on**: None.
close_reason: convergence_perm: ds₁ ~ ds₂ → foldl apply_tx produces same store. Via foldl_apply_tx_union + toFinset_eq_of_perm

title: BUG: transact() does not guarantee strict growth — no tx metadata datoms (DEFECT-I-007)
description:
**Observed**: If a committed transaction contains only datoms already in the store, transact() succeeds and increments epoch, but store.len() is unchanged. BTreeSet::insert for duplicates is a no-op. No tx metadata datoms are created.
**Expected**: INV-FERR-004: every transaction adds at least its tx_entity metadata datoms, ensuring strict growth. Spec says |transact(S, T)| > |S|.
**Reproducer**: Transact a committed tx whose datoms are already in the store — len() before == len() after.
**Root cause**: tx metadata datoms (recording the transaction itself as a datom) are not yet implemented. This is Phase 4a-SCHEMA (bd-85j.11) scope.
**Affected INV**: INV-FERR-004
**File(s)**: ferratomic-core/src/store.rs
**Fix**: Add tx metadata datom creation in transact() — at minimum a datom recording (tx_entity, :tx/time, Instant(now), tx_id, Assert). Blocked by schema having :tx/time attribute (bd-85j.11).
**Depends on**: bd-85j.11
close_reason: Fixed by bd-85j.11: transact() now creates tx/time and tx/agent metadata datoms, guaranteeing strict growth. INV-FERR-004.

title: Fix genesis_deterministic Lean tautology (CR-001)
description:
**What**: genesis_deterministic proves x = x (rfl). Since genesis_model is defined as ∅, even restating as genesis_model = ∅ would be rfl. Delete the vacuous theorem — genesis_bottom, genesis_merge_left, genesis_merge_right already prove all substantive properties of genesis.
**Why**: INV-FERR-031. Theorem inflates coverage count without proof content. The 3 sibling theorems are sufficient.
**Acceptance**:
1. genesis_deterministic deleted from Performance.lean.
2. Comment added: "-- Genesis properties: genesis_bottom (bottom element), genesis_merge_left/right (identity element)".
3. lake build succeeds.
**File(s)**: ferratomic-verify/lean/Ferratomic/Performance.lean
**Depends on**: None.
